title: melaleuca linearifolia
intro: Melaleuca linearifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved melaleuca, is a plant species native to the subtropical regions of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Melaleuca linearifolia can be found in various forms, such as essential oils, extracts, and tea infusions, which are often used to promote wound healing and support immune function.
